laws.title: Public Law 3-21 | Effective Judicial Function and General Duties Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Generally applicable judicial-administration statute with no self-repeal, sunset, or superseding clause. It creates continuing duties, reporting obligations, and an immediate effective date.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and enactment label for the act.
provisions.evidence: "This bill may be cited as \"S. 14\""

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause and anti-supersession language.
provisions.evidence: "This Resolution shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Chief Justice duties and administrative powers over the Federal Judiciary.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new Title 28 section for Chief Justice administrative duties and authority to ensure court business is attended with proper dispatch.
provisions.evidence: "The Chief Justice shall see that the business of the several courts of the Federal Government is attended with proper dispatch"

provisions.text_summary: Reporting requirements to Congress, the President, and the Judicial Conference.
provisions.exact_change: Create a companion Title 28 reporting provision requiring reports after action under SEC 3.
provisions.evidence: "he shall submit a report with the Senate and House Committees on the Judiciary"

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date after ratification and signature.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall take effect immediately"

current_implementation.detail: A boilerplate statutory note with a full quoted-content dump was placed at Title 50. The act is substantive, generally applicable judicial-administration law and should be enacted as real Code text in Title 28 rather than as a note.

recommended_actions: Remove the Title 50 note treatment and codify the Chief Justice duties and reporting rules as actual Title 28 text.
Strip the Trello archive link and keep only a concise statutory note if the enactment is still being represented as historical context.
Do not preserve the full quoted-content dump in permanent text.

provisions.text_summary: No statutory text recoverable; only Drive page debris is available.
provisions.evidence: "Copy of District of Columbia City Charter (ammended) - Google Drive"
provisions.notes: source-limited; no operative language was recoverable.

recommended_actions: Retain only as a source-limited historical note if needed; do not assert substantive status from the debris page.
Remove the Trello link from rp-pl003022-codification.
Re-acquire the authentic enactment before any codification work.

laws.title: Public Law 3-24 | Judgeship Reducation Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The act imposes ongoing structural rules for federal courts and expressly repeals earlier legislation; it does not self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This piece of legislation shall be referred to as the Judgeship Reduction Act of 2022"

provisions.text_summary: Reduce the Supreme Court to one Chief Justice and four Associate Justices.
provisions.exact_change: Create or amend Title 28 structural text to set the Supreme Court's membership at 1 Chief Justice and 4 Associate Justices.
provisions.evidence: "The available number of seats on the United States Supreme Court shall be one (1) Chief Justice and four (4) Associate Justices"

provisions.text_summary: Permanently close the United States Court of Appeals.
provisions.exact_change: Mark the affected appellate-court structure as repealed/abolished and remove any conflicting positive-law text.
provisions.evidence: "The United States Court of Appeals (in any and all circuits) shall be permanently closed"

provisions.text_summary: Set District of Columbia district-court staffing and make it the only federal district court.
provisions.exact_change: Create or amend Title 28 text to reflect the reduced D.C. district-court structure and exclusivity claim.
provisions.evidence: "The United States District Court for the District of Columbia shall be the only federal District Court"

provisions.text_summary: Set the number of magistrates to three.
provisions.exact_change: Add or amend Title 28 text governing magistrate staffing to a three-magistrate rule.
provisions.evidence: "The available number of Magistrates to serve under the United States District Court ... shall be three (3)"

provisions.text_summary: Applicability savings clause preserving incumbents already in office.
provisions.evidence: "This statute shall not be applied in a manner that shall remove or otherwise affect any person currently a Justice"

provisions.text_summary: Express repeal and supersession of prior acts and conflicting law.
provisions.exact_change: Mark the referenced prior enactments as superseded/repealed to the extent they remain operative.
provisions.evidence: "The Court of Appeals Temporary Suspension Act of 2021 is hereby superseded and repealed in full"

current_implementation.detail: The XML contains only a boilerplate note with a full quoted-content dump. The act makes structural changes and repeal instructions that require real positive-law treatment, not a note.

recommended_actions: Replace the note with actual Title 28 text or explicit repeal markings for the affected court-structure provisions.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link from the permanent XML note.
If any structural clause is not codifiable, isolate it as a short historical note instead of keeping the whole enactment in one note.

laws.title: Public Law 3-25 | CRACK Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. This is a large, generally applicable codification and criminal-law reform bill with multiple direct amendments and new chapters/sections. It does not self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and table of contents.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the \"Countering, Restricting, and other Appropriate Controls for Killers (CRACK) Act of 2022\""

provisions.text_summary: Construction and severability clause.
provisions.evidence: "Any provision of this Act held to be invalid or unenforceable... shall be deemed severable from this Act"

provisions.text_summary: Repeal of the National Security Act of 2021.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal Titles I-IV of the prior National Security Act of 2021.
provisions.evidence: "The National Security Act of 2021 shall be repealed in its entirety"

provisions.text_summary: Create and govern the National Security Council, threat-designation process, intelligence access, and communications limits.
provisions.exact_change: Add new Title 50 NSC governance and oversight text, including council functions, threat-designation procedures, intelligence access, and communications rules.
provisions.evidence: "The National Security Council shall" / "The Chief Justice shall" is not present; the act instead says "The function of the Council shall be"

provisions.text_summary: Amend 18 U.S.C. 2510 and 3127 to define terrorist activities and criminal investigation.
provisions.exact_change: Redesignate 18 U.S.C. 2510(20)-(21) as (22)-(23) and insert new definitions after paragraph (19); also insert 'terrorist activities' and 'criminal investigation' into 18 U.S.C. 3127(1).
provisions.evidence: "Section2510oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamended"

provisions.text_summary: Reserved section with no operative text.
provisions.evidence: "SECTION151.RESERVED.Reserved."

provisions.text_summary: Reserved section with no operative text.
provisions.evidence: "SECTION152.RESERVED.Reserved."

provisions.text_summary: Add a new administrative-subpoena section for terrorism investigations.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new 18 U.S.C. 2332j after section 2332i in chapter 113B.
provisions.evidence: "Chapter113Boftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbyinsertingaftersection2332ithefollowing"

provisions.text_summary: Replace the non-disclosure penalty provision for national-security requests.
provisions.exact_change: Strike the existing 18 U.S.C. 1510(e) text and replace it with the new penalty language listed in the act.
provisions.evidence: "Section1510(e)oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbystrikingtheexistingprovisionandreplacingwith"

provisions.text_summary: Add a judicial-enforcement section for information requests tied to national-security letters.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new 18 U.S.C. 2332k before section 2333.
provisions.evidence: "Chapter113Boftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbyinsertingbeforesection2333thefollowing"

provisions.text_summary: Definitions, classification standards, classification levels, authority, and classification categories for the national-security title.
provisions.exact_change: Add new Title 50 classification framework text covering definitions, standards, levels, authority, and categories.
provisions.evidence: "SECTION201.DEFINITIONS"

provisions.text_summary: Duration, markings, prohibitions, Attorney General action, challenges, derivative classification, guides, declassification, safeguarding information, and special access programs.
provisions.exact_change: Add the remaining Title 50 classification-management rules, including time limits, identification, challenge procedures, and special-access-program safeguards.
provisions.evidence: "SECTION206.DURATIONOFCLASSIFICATION"

provisions.text_summary: Protect detainee identity, protect Capitol-building information, and tighten ex parte procedure under CIPA.
provisions.exact_change: Add new non-disclosure protections overriding 5 U.S.C. 552 and amend 18 U.S.C. App. 3 section 4 by replacing 'may' with 'shall'.
provisions.evidence: "Notwithstandingsection552oftitle5,UnitedStatesCode" / "Section4oftheClassifiedInformationProceduresAct(18U.S.C.App.3)isherebyamended"

provisions.text_summary: Short title for the information-sharing subtitle.
provisions.evidence: "This Subtitle may be cited as the \"Information Sharing and Cooperation Act of 2022\""

provisions.text_summary: Remedies for surveillance litigation and immunity for voluntary disclosures to federal law enforcement.
provisions.exact_change: Create standalone remedies and disclosure-immunity provisions for law-enforcement surveillance matters.
provisions.evidence: "SECTION302.APPROPRIATEREMEDIESWITHRESPECTTOLAWENFORCEMENTSURVEILLANCEACTIVITIES"

provisions.text_summary: Short title for the terrorism-crimes subtitle.
provisions.evidence: "This title may be cited as the \"Protecting Against Terrorism Act of 2022\""

provisions.text_summary: Create the terrorism-hoax offense and expand material-support / terrorism definitions.
provisions.exact_change: Insert 18 U.S.C. 1041 after section 1040 and amend 2339A, 2331, and 2339B to expand the terrorism material-support provisions.
provisions.evidence: "Chapter47oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbyinsertingaftersection1040thefollowing" / "Section2339A(a)oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedby"

provisions.text_summary: Add encrypted-communications offense, terrorism detention presumption, and post-release supervision rules.
provisions.exact_change: Insert chapter 124 with section 2801, amend 18 U.S.C. 3142, and amend 18 U.S.C. 3583.
provisions.evidence: "PartIoftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedbyinsertingafterchapter123thefollowing" / "Section3142oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamended" / "Section3583oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamended"

provisions.text_summary: Suspend/revoke/deny security certificates for national-security reasons, eliminate limitations periods for terrorism crimes, and add terrorist-murder penalties.
provisions.exact_change: Amend 18 U.S.C. 3286, add new 2339E penalties, and cross-reference the new penalty in 18 U.S.C. 3592.
provisions.evidence: "Section3286(b)oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamended" / "Section3592(c)(1)oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamended"

provisions.text_summary: Correct the financing-of-terrorism statute.
provisions.exact_change: Revise 18 U.S.C. 2339C(c)(2) and (e) as specified in the act.
provisions.evidence: "Section2339C(c)(2)oftitle18,UnitedStatesCode,isamendedby"

provisions.text_summary: Define criminal intelligence, require a national plan, create a coordination council, and set criminal-intelligence safeguarding rules.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new criminal-intelligence governance subtitle to Title 50 or the closest subject-matter anchor.
provisions.evidence: "SECTION501.DEFINITIONS"

provisions.text_summary: Create a RICO-style chapter, define racketeering terms, prohibit racketeering activities, and set criminal penalties.
provisions.exact_change: Supersede Chapter 96 and replace it with the new racketeering chapter text.
provisions.evidence: "Thistitlemaybecitedasthe\"RacketeeringInfluencedandCorruptOrganizationsActof2022\"andshallsupersede18U.S.CodeChapter96"

provisions.text_summary: Civil remedies, venue, expedition, evidence, and civil investigative demand procedures for the RICO-style chapter.
provisions.exact_change: Add the civil-enforcement and procedure provisions that follow the new chapter text.
provisions.evidence: "SECTION605.CIVILREMEDIES" / "SECTION609.CIVILINVESTIGATIVEDEMAND"

provisions.text_summary: Archivist compilation duty, capital-district security commission, and immediate enactment.
provisions.evidence: "TheArchivistoftheUnitedStatesshallpublishandcompileallaffectedsectionsoftheUnitedStatesCodeasamended" / "ThisActshallgointoeffectimmediatelyuponpassage"

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is a boilerplate statutory note with a Trello link, but the enactment contains many express strike/insert amendments and new sections that require actual textual integration.

recommended_actions: Replace the note-only handling with real textual amendments in the affected titles and chapters.
Keep only concise statutory history for uncodified administrative directions and reserved sections.
Remove the Trello archive link and avoid preserving the act as a single dumped note.

laws.title: Public Law 4-26 | H.J. Res. 06 (Keywords: Tigray)
laws.status_basis: The source is a resolution that declares a state of war and contains no self-repeal or sunset. It is non-code in character but not expired on its face.

provisions.text_summary: Declaration that a state of war exists between the United States and the Nation of Tigray.
provisions.evidence: "a state of war exists between the United States and the Nation of Tigray"
provisions.notes: resolution; no operative Code amendment target.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note treats the resolution as repealed or superseded history, but the source is a live declaration of war with no repeal language. The placement can be historical only if kept, but the status label is wrong.

recommended_actions: Keep only a brief historical note if the archive needs one; do not classify the resolution as repealed or superseded without support.
Remove the Trello link.
Do not treat this as permanent positive-law text.

laws.title: Public Law 4-27 | Right to Security Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The act creates substantive general rules on private security, self-defense, and cooperation with law enforcement, and it does not self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This piece of legislation shall be referred to as \"Right to Security Act of 2022\""

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for individual, private security, and federal protection services.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new general-law section containing the act's defined terms and applying the definitions across the act.
provisions.evidence: "\"Private Security\" shall refer to any organization or firm licensed by the U.S. Department of Commerce"

provisions.text_summary: Purpose clause and limitations governing private security and self-defense conduct.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the substantive rights and limitations for private security, individuals, and castle-doctrine style protections.
provisions.evidence: "Protecting all federally licensed Private Security companies from prosecution"

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is only a boilerplate placeholder with a quoted-content dump. The act is substantive general law and should be expressed as real code text or a carefully scoped historical note, not as a single note.

recommended_actions: Replace the note placeholder with actual text for the defined terms and substantive rules.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello archive link.
If any clause is left uncodified, keep only a concise historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 4-28 | House Veterans Affairs Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The act is a substantive general veterans policy bill with continuing programmatic obligations and no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This piece of legislation shall be referred to as the Department of Veterans Affairs Act of 2022"

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for veteran and honorably discharged.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new Title 38 definitions section for the act's veterans terminology.
provisions.evidence: "Veteran - An individual who has been honorably discharged from the Armed Forces of the United States of America."

provisions.text_summary: Short title for the House-resolution label and Department of Veterans Affairs reference.
provisions.evidence: "This Bill may be cited or referred to as House Resolution 4003 or the Department of Veterans Affairs Act."

provisions.text_summary: Equal-employment opportunities and career-shortening provisions for veterans.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the veteran-employment assistance and training-shortcut rules as positive law.

provisions.text_summary: Social, legal, mental-health, and career-assistance benefits for veterans.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the benefits and counseling program rules as actual veterans-law text.

Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 4-29 | Department of Commerce and Labor Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The act establishes a new executive department, transfers agency functions, and sets ongoing duties. It does not self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This piece of legislation shall be referred to as Department of Commerce and Labor Act of 2022"

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for Labor, Commerce, Secretary of Commerce and Labor, and President.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new statutory definitions section for the Commerce-and-Labor reorganization.
provisions.evidence: "Department of Labor shall be defined as the closed department pursuant to Senate Bill 2 §(2)(c)"

provisions.text_summary: Transfer and consolidation of departments, agencies, assets, liabilities, and offices into the new department.
provisions.exact_change: Transfer the listed agencies and assets into the new Department of Commerce and Labor and consolidate the Commerce and Labor portfolios.
provisions.evidence: "The United States Department of Labor is hereby allocated under the Department of Commerce and Labor"

provisions.text_summary: Department powers, responsibilities, and reporting lines.
provisions.exact_change: Add the department's general powers, internal organization, and reporting structure as actual statutory text.
provisions.evidence: "The Department of Commerce and Labor is hereby allocated and designated the following responsibilities and powers"

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is a broad statutory-note placeholder with a full quoted-content dump. The act creates a new department and transfers agencies, so the implementation needs real structural text instead of a note.

recommended_actions: Convert the note into actual structural text for the new department and transfer rules.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello archive link.
Keep only the short title and other uncodified housekeeping language as concise notes if necessary.

laws.title: Public Law 4-30 | Fugitive Justice Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The act creates a fugitive-list regime and expressly repeals H.R. 4; it does not repeal itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and enactment label.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the \"Fugitive Justice ReformAct\""

provisions.text_summary: Purpose clause.
provisions.evidence: "It is the purpose of this act to reform Fugitive Justice"

provisions.text_summary: Effective-on-passage enactment language.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be enacted once passed through both chambers of Congress and signed by the President of the United States"

provisions.text_summary: Defines fugitive status and creates the fugitive list regime.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new fugitive-list and absentia-trial regime in the closest subject-matter title if retained as positive law.
provisions.evidence: "they shall be considered to be a Fugitive"

provisions.text_summary: Warrant database duties for the U.S. Marshal Services.
provisions.exact_change: Add the warrant-database obligations as a new statutory administration rule if codified.
provisions.evidence: "The United States Marshal Services shall maintain a Warrant Database"

provisions.text_summary: Removal mechanisms from the fugitive list.
provisions.evidence: "There shall be four mechanisms for removal from The Fugitive List"

provisions.text_summary: Repeal of H.R. 4 and all amendments ratified to it.
provisions.exact_change: Mark the referenced H.R. 4 enactment as repealed and rescinded in the prior-law record.
provisions.evidence: "H. R. 4 in its entirety ... shall be repealed and rescinded"

current_implementation.detail: The note treats the act as repealed or superseded history, but the source is an operative enactment that creates a fugitive-list regime and repeals an earlier bill. The XML should preserve the statute as current law rather than historical-only status.

recommended_actions: Treat the act as current operative law and remove any blanket repealed/superseded label.
Retain only concise statutory history for the non-code sections and move the substantive rules into proper positive-law text if they are to be codified.
Remove the Trello link from the XML note.
